headline: Rising gas prices could offset any tax refunds for US consumers
summary: US consumers may be in for a surprise as the Iran war stirs up price shocks for oil (CL=F, BZ=F) and gas (RB=F), ultimately limiting Americans' spending power for discretionary purchases and offsetting the benefits of tax refunds. Yahoo Finance Senior Reporter Brooke DiPalma comes on Market Domination to outline the impact of added geopolitical pressures on consumers. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Domination.

headline: How $200 Oil Impacts Your Stocks and Your Wallet

headline: Alimentaria+Hostelco 2026 reinforces its leadership in a historic edition
summary: Alimentaria+Hostelco will return to Fira de Barcelona's Gran Via venue from 23 to 26 March 2026, consolidating its position as a leading platform for the food, hospitality and foodservice value chain. In an edition marking Alimentaria's 50th anniversary, the event will span 100,000 m² in seven pavilions, with international participation up 41% compared to 2024.

headline: How Store Expansion, New Brands, and Governance Moves At Walmart (WMT) Has Changed Its Investment Story
summary: In early March 2026, Walmart opened a new Supercenter in The Villages, FL, continued rolling out new formats nationwide, and was chosen as the exclusive or primary mass‑retail partner for several new consumer brands, while also appointing former U.S. Attorney Erin Nealy Cox as its future Executive Vice President of Global Governance, Chief Legal Officer, and Corporate Secretary, effective April 13, 2026. Together, the store expansion, influx of differentiated beauty and beverage offerings...
